figures.caption: Figure 4: Evaluation suites used in this work: the LIBERO and MetaWorld MT50 simulation benchmarks, and a real Piper robot arm.
Table 1: Main results on MetaWorld MT50. Success rates (%) across difficulty tiers and planning horizons H . ∆π0 and ∆π0.5 denote the absolute gain over π0 and π0.5.

Table 13: Inference-time horizon sweep on LIBERO-Long, using the same model weights (trained at AH=20). Tuning only the inference horizon and the replan step moves the success rate from 93.4% to 95.6%.
Table 9: Task difficulty partition for MetaWorld MT50, following [36].

Figure 2: The OneWM-VLA Framework. Through Adaptive Attention Pooling (Adaptive Fusion), we distill multi-view visual features into a dense latent world token for each perspective. These tokens are aligned with action trajectories via Joint Flow Matching to ensure alignment between environment dynamics transitions and robot control.

Figure 5: PCA visualization of visual features on LIBERO-Long. Top: before pooling (256 tokens, mean-aggregated to a single vector per sample). Bottom: after Adaptive Attention Pooling (1 token per view per frame). The class-level structure remains visually separable after pooling.
